1: Endow the king with Your justice, O God,and the son of the king with Your righteousness.
2: May he judge Your people with righteousnessand Your afflicted with justice.
3: May the mountains bring peace to the people,and the hills bring righteousness.
4: May he vindicate the afflicted among the people;may he save the children of the needyand crush the oppressor.
5: May they fear himas long as the sun shines,as long as the moon remains,through all generations.
6: May he be like rain that falls on freshly cut grass,like spring showers that water the earth.
7: May the righteous flourish in his daysand prosperity abounduntil the moon is no more.
8: May he rule from sea to sea,and from the Euphratesto the ends of the earth.
9: May the nomads bow before him,and his enemies lick the dust.
10: May the kings of Tarshish and distant shores bring tribute;may the kings of Sheba and Seba offer gifts.
11: May all kings bow down to himand all nations serve him.
12: For he will deliver the needy who cry outand the afflicted who have no helper.
13: He will take pity on the poor and needyand save the lives of the oppressed.
14: He will redeem them from oppression and violence,for their blood is precious in his sight.
15: Long may he live!May gold from Sheba be given him.May people ever pray for him;may they bless him all day long.
16: May there be an abundance of grain in the land;may it sway atop the hills.May its fruit trees flourish like the forests of Lebanon,the people of its cities like the grass of the field.
17: May his name endure forever;may his name continueas long as the sun shines.In him may all nations be blessed;may they call him blessed.
18: Blessed be the LORD God, the God of Israel,who alone does marvelous deeds.
19: And blessed be His glorious name forever;may all the earth be filled with His glory.Amen and amen.
20: Thus conclude the prayers of David son of Jesse.
